evals.prompt: review my changes, I added user authentication to the Express app
evals.expected_output: A structured code review summary identifying security issues (plaintext password storage, SQL injection), missing input validation, and suggesting improvements like bcrypt and parameterized queries.

assertions.text: Review identifies the SQL injection vulnerability from string-interpolated queries
Review identifies that passwords are stored in plaintext without hashing
Review flags the hardcoded database password 'admin123'
Review notes that the login response returns the full user object including password
Output uses a structured report format with severity-based sections (critical/warning/suggestion)

evals.prompt: does this look right? I was fixing a race condition in the job queue
evals.expected_output: A code review identifying that the race condition fix is incomplete (check-then-act is still non-atomic), suggesting proper locking or atomic operations.

assertions.text: Review identifies that claim_next_job does not hold the lock during the check-then-act sequence
Review notes that add_job is not thread-safe (list append and id assignment are unprotected)
Review notes that complete_job and fail_job also lack locking
Output uses a structured report format with severity-based sections (critical/warning/suggestion)

evals.prompt: codex review this - I implemented a thread-safe cache in Rust
evals.expected_output: A code review identifying the TOCTOU race in get_or_insert, the unnecessary unsafe impl Send/Sync, the access_count never being incremented, and the cleanup thread holding the lock forever blocking other operations.

assertions.text: Review identifies the TOCTOU race condition in get_or_insert where another thread can insert between the get check and the set call
Review flags the manual unsafe impl Send/Sync as unnecessary since Arc<Mutex<_>> already provides these guarantees
Review notes that access_count is initialized to 0 but never incremented on get, making the eviction policy broken
Review identifies that start_cleanup_thread holds the mutex lock in an infinite loop with sleep, blocking all other cache operations during sleep
Output uses a structured report format with severity-based sections (critical/warning/suggestion)
